acceptance_targets: [CH12_CRAFT_PROOF_GAP] Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:293 gives the reader one concrete observed event that verifies a specific claim about the System or the bowl, explicitly marks at least one remaining point as hypothesis rather than fact, and makes Maren's decision at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:273 follow from that observed change. Fail if the containment model still rests almost entirely on Aldric's explanation.

[CH12_DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_REVEAL] Pass if the revised dialogue in the highlighted spans contains at least two Maren lines that visibly self-correct, trail off, or redirect instead of resolving into tidy thesis statements, and at least one Aldric explanation is interrupted, revised, or forced into a shorter reactive answer by scene pressure. Fail if the main reveal beats still read chiefly as quotable diagnosis followed by neat confirmation.

[CH12_PROSE_SAT_SCAFFOLD] Pass if no more than one of the cited moments still uses the exact sat in/sat with scaffold for internal pressure, and the opening hum, mid-chapter processing beat, and ending with the bowl each produce a distinct bodily or environmental image on the page. Fail if multiple key beats still rely on the same resting-placement construction.
[R-04] The bowl reference at line 3102 does not restate its duration of use or its status as a prop.
[length_chapter_12_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:293 gives the reader one concrete observed event that verifies a specific claim about the System or the bowl, explicitly marks at least one remaining point as hypothesis rather than fact, and makes Maren's decision at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:273 follow from that observed change. Fail if the containment model still rests almost entirely on Aldric's explanation.
findings.problem: The chapter's paradigm shift is legible as thesis, but too much of it arrives as Aldric's abstract briefing rather than as something the scene itself proves. Wall, containment, widening breach, and the bowl's function are all stated clearly enough; what is missing is a stronger immediate on-page test that lets Maren and the reader verify one of those claims before his Crook-first decision. The result is a reveal scene that explains the new metaphysics more than it dramatizes them.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the explanatory runs beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:115, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:141 so at least one major claim about containment, breach behavior, or ritual function is demonstrated by an immediate sensory or physical change in the campsite before Maren sets terms at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:273 and the bowl reacts at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:293. Strategy options: have Maren deliberately test the bowl or the hum, force Aldric and Vess to distinguish proof from inference in real time, or make the environment answer one specific question instead of leaving every key mechanic in report language.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised dialogue in the highlighted spans contains at least two Maren lines that visibly self-correct, trail off, or redirect instead of resolving into tidy thesis statements, and at least one Aldric explanation is interrupted, revised, or forced into a shorter reactive answer by scene pressure. Fail if the main reveal beats still read chiefly as quotable diagnosis followed by neat confirmation.
findings.problem: The pressure-scene dialogue is too often composed into thesis speech and mini-speech diagnosis. Maren gets several clean, quotable grievance summaries, and Aldric's most important explanations arrive as finished doctrine rather than as speech under strain. That polish blunts the style-bible contrast the scene wants: overwhelmed Maren should get rougher, more self-interrupting, and more concrete, while frightened Aldric should sound formal but less serenely complete than he does here.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchanges centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:219, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:279. Keep Aldric's low-contraction formality, but break some of his doctrine into more reactive answers, interruption, or revision under pressure. Let Maren lose finish: self-correct, restart, redirect, or drop into concrete fragments when he gets close to the real grievance instead of consistently landing polished accounting lines.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than one of the cited moments still uses the exact sat in/sat with scaffold for internal pressure, and the opening hum, mid-chapter processing beat, and ending with the bowl each produce a distinct bodily or environmental image on the page. Fail if multiple key beats still rely on the same resting-placement construction.
findings.problem: A marked bodily-pressure scaffold recurs mechanically across the chapter: the hum sat at a pitch, then sat in Maren's teeth and molars, then the revelation sat in his chest, then he sat with it, then he sat with the bowl and the terms. Because the same resting-placement construction keeps returning, different emotional and sensory beats start to flatten into one default sentence shape instead of feeling freshly embodied.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated constructions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:157, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:297 so the opening hum, the processing pause, and the closing bowl beat each register through different tactile, proprioceptive, or environmental language. Keeping one instance as motif is fine; the problem is the chapter-wide reuse.

findings.acceptance_test: The bowl reference at line 3102 does not restate its duration of use or its status as a prop.
findings.evidence: Ch12 line 3102: 'Same chipped rim. Same market glaze. Same stupid prop he'd used for eleven years.' By Ch12 the phrase has appeared 39 times in prior chapters.
findings.problem: The bowl-as-prop baseline and eleven-year duration are re-proved in Ch12 narration despite 39 prior uses. First established Ch1 line 9; restated in Ch2 line 388, Ch5 line 966, and elsewhere.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut 'he'd used for eleven years' from the bowl description. The 'chipped rim' and 'market glaze' already identify the bowl. If the fraud context is needed, replace with a forward-looking beat.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
